LOOK.text: A sterile white reception desk. A sign-in sheet sits on the counter. The names... 'Ser Rug M.', 'HODLer Anonymous', 'Diamond H.', 'Ser Liquidated III'. These people need help.

USE.text: I ring the bell on the desk. A hollow ding echoes through the empty reception. Nobody comes. Par for the course in healthcare AND crypto.

TALK.text: I lean over the desk and whisper: 'Hello?' The echo comes back: 'NGMI...'

then.text: I already took the sign-in sheet. The desk looks naked without it.

else.text: I grab the sign-in sheet. Patient names, admission dates, diagnoses... 'Chronic Airdrop Dependency', 'Acute Rug Pull Trauma', 'Terminal FOMO'. This is gold.

OPEN.text: I try the desk drawers. Locked. A small sign says: 'Patient confidentiality is our top priority. Also we lost the key.'

CLOSE.text: The drawers are already closed. Probably for the best.

PUSH.text: I push the bell again. Ding. Still nobody. This place has worse response times than Ethereum mainnet on a mint day.

PULL.text: I pull the sign-in sheet closer. The last entry is dated today. Someone checked in recently.

GIVE.text: The reception desk doesn't accept walk-in donations. Only scheduled appointments and insurance co-pays in ETH.

LOOK.text: Motivational posters line the wall. 'ONE DAY AT A BLOCK.' 'SERENITY PRAYER: Grant me the serenity to accept the trades I cannot reverse, the courage to HODL the ones I can, and the wisdom to know the difference.' Powerful stuff.

USE.text: I straighten one of the posters. Behind it, someone scratched into the wall: 'WAGMI was a lie.' Dark.

TALK.text: I read the posters aloud. 'THE 12 STEPS OF CRYPTO RECOVERY: Step 1 - Admit you are powerless over leverage.' I stop at Step 2. It just says 'There is no Step 2.'

PICK.text: I try to peel a poster off. It's glued on tight. The clinic takes its motivational content seriously.

OPEN.text: I look behind a poster. Just the wall. And the faint scent of broken dreams.

CLOSE.text: I smooth the poster back. 'KEEP CALM AND STOP MARKET BUYING.' Words to live by.

PUSH.text: I push on the poster. The wall behind it is solid. Unlike most token whitepapers.

PULL.text: I pull the corner of a poster. It tears slightly, revealing another poster underneath: 'BUY THE DIP.' Old habits die hard.

GIVE.text: The posters give ME motivation. I don't need to give them anything except my attention.

LOOK.text: Uncomfortable plastic chairs. Old magazines scattered on a side table: 'CoinDesk, March 2022: Everything is Fine', 'Degen Weekly: Top 10 Rugs of the Year', and a well-worn copy of 'Blockchain for Dummies' with all the pages torn out.

USE.text: I sit down. The chair squeaks. I flip through 'CoinDesk March 2022'. Every headline aged like milk. Beautiful.

TALK.text: I mutter to the empty chairs: 'So... come here often?' Silence. Toughest crowd since my last Twitter Space.

PICK.text: I check under the chair cushions. A crumpled receipt for 10,000 LUNA. Oh no.

OPEN.text: I open one of the magazines. An article titled 'Why This Bull Market Will Never End' from November 2021. Narrator: It ended.

CLOSE.text: I close the magazine. Some articles are better left unfinished. Like most ICO roadmaps.

PUSH.text: I push a chair aside. It scrapes the floor with a sound that could wake the dead. Or at least a sleeping night nurse.

PULL.text: I pull a chair closer. Might as well get comfortable. The wait times here are probably denominated in blocks.

GIVE.text: The chairs have received enough. Mostly butts and bad news. Same as my portfolio.

LOOK.text: A tall metal filing cabinet. A lock keeps it sealed. The label reads 'PATIENT RECORDS - CONFIDENTIAL'. The files inside could explain everything about Patient Zero.

USE.text: The cabinet is locked. I'd need a key to access these files. HIPAA compliance meets private key security.

TALK.text: I whisper to the cabinet: 'What secrets do you hold?' It says nothing. Better at keeping secrets than most Discord mods.

PICK.text: The cabinet weighs a ton. Full of records. And probably full of stories I'm not ready to hear.

OPEN.text: Locked. Needs a key. The files inside could explain everything about Patient Zero's condition. And maybe... my own.

CLOSE.text: It's already closed. And locked. Fort Knox has nothing on this filing cabinet.

PUSH.text: I push the cabinet. It doesn't budge. It's either bolted down or full of every bad trade ever recorded.

PULL.text: I pull on the drawer handle. Locked tight. The rattle echoes through the empty reception.

GIVE.text: The cabinet only accepts keys. And judging by the lock, very specific keys.

LOOK.text: A heavy door labeled 'TREATMENT ROOM - AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY'. A card reader blinks red beside it. Whatever's inside, they don't want visitors.

lines: The treatment room. One chair. One monitor.
Dr. Satoshi said not everyone is ready for what's inside.
...has been in your wallet all along.

else.text: The door is sealed. The card reader blinks red. I need proper authorization to get through here.

TALK.text: I press my ear against the door. Faint beeping from the other side. Like a heart monitor. Or a liquidation alert.

PICK.text: I can't pick up a door. Especially one this reinforced. It's like trying to move a whale wallet.

lines: The treatment room. One chair. One monitor.
Dr. Satoshi said not everyone is ready for what's inside.
...has been in your wallet all along.

else.text: Locked. The card reader demands authorization. No brute force attacks allowed.

CLOSE.text: The door is already closed. And very much wants to stay that way.

PUSH.text: I push on the door. Solid steel. It laughs at my attempt. Not literally. That would be terrifying.

PULL.text: I pull the handle. The card reader buzzes angrily. 'ACCESS DENIED.' Story of my DeFi career.

GIVE.text: The door only accepts authorized keycards. Not bribes. This isn't a governance vote.

LOOK.text: The water cooler bubbles rhythmically. Someone taped a sign to it: 'This water is NOT tokenized. Do NOT try to fractionalize it. Management.'

USE.text: I pour myself a cup. Cold, refreshing, and completely free. A concept foreign to the crypto space.

TALK.text: The water cooler gurgles. Classic water cooler talk. The original social protocol.

PICK.text: I grab a paper cup. It has the clinic's logo: a wallet with a band-aid on it.

OPEN.text: I open the spigot. Water flows. No gas fees required.

CLOSE.text: I close the spigot. The cooler continues its eternal bubbling. Proof-of-hydration.

PUSH.text: I push the cold water button. A satisfying gurgle. If only yield farming was this straightforward.

PULL.text: I pull the hot water lever. Steam rises. Someone labeled it 'ALTSEASON.' Appropriate.

GIVE.text: The water cooler provides. It doesn't receive. The purest form of decentralized hydration.

LOOK.text: A faded photograph. A group of people at a ribbon cutting. 'PZRC Opening Day — Block Genesis'. One face is scratched out.

else.text: I carefully remove the photo from the frame. The scratched-out face... who was it? And why erase them?

USE.text: I hold the photo up to the light. The scratched-out face is too damaged to identify. Whoever did this really committed to the delete.

TALK.text: 'Who are you?' I ask the scratched-out face. The photo says nothing. It's been holding this secret since Block 0.

OPEN.text: I flip the photo over. On the back: 'Grand Opening — The future of recovery starts here.' Someone crossed out 'future' and wrote 'end.'

CLOSE.text: I put the photo face-down. Some faces are better left scratched out. Especially in crypto.

PUSH.text: I push the photo frame straight. It tilts back immediately. Even the decor here refuses to stay aligned.

PULL.text: I pull the frame off the wall. Behind it: a small nail hole and a faint rectangle of unfaded paint. This photo has been here a long time.

GIVE.text: The photo has already given me more questions than answers. The worst kind of airdrop.

else.text: His coat's inner pocket... a vintage badge: 'Dr. S. Nakamoto — Chief of Rehabilitation, Block 0'. This is a collector's item.

then.text: Wait... there's something in the inner pocket. A glint of metal.
The coat has an embroidered name tag: 'S. Nakamoto'. The stitching is impeccable.

else.text: A pristine white coat. Dr. Satoshi takes his role seriously.

USE.text: I shouldn't mess with the doctor's coat while he's wearing it.

TALK.text: I'm not going to talk to a coat. I have standards. Low ones, but still.

PICK.text: I consider stealing the doctor's coat. Then I remember the Hippocratic Oath probably covers theft of medical apparel.

OPEN.text: I open the coat pockets one by one. A pen, a stethoscope, and a sticky note that says 'Remember: we're all Patient Zero.'

CLOSE.text: I button up the coat. It looks better closed. Like a short position before earnings.

PUSH.text: I push the coat aside on its hook. It swings back. The coat has better rebalancing than my portfolio.

PULL.text: I pull the coat toward me. It smells like antiseptic and regret. The official scent of crypto rehabilitation.

GIVE.text: I'm not giving anything to a coat. It already has more pockets than my wallet has tokens.

LOOK.text: Flickering fluorescent tubes. One of them buzzes like a failing validator node. The harsh light makes everything look sickly, which is on-brand for a rehab clinic.

USE.text: I flick the switch. Nothing changes. These lights have one mode: soul-crushing institutional glow.

TALK.text: I address the buzzing light: 'You sound like my GPU mining rig in 2021.' It flickers in solidarity.

PICK.text: I'm not tall enough to reach them. And even if I were, stealing lights from a rehab clinic is a new low. Even for a degen.

OPEN.text: The light fixture is sealed shut. Tamper-proof, like a properly audited smart contract.

CLOSE.text: The lights don't have an off switch I can reach. Always on. Like the blockchain.

PUSH.text: I push up toward the lights. My hands wave uselessly in the air. This is how I look trying to time the market.

PULL.text: I jump and try to pull the fixture. I miss by a mile. My vertical is as weak as my exit strategy.

GIVE.text: The lights don't need anything from me. They're already burning through electricity like an Ethereum PoW miner.

LOOK.text: A poster with a glowing red lightbulb. Below it reads: 'BRIGHT IDEAS START WITH UNPLUGGING.' The irony of a clinic for screen addicts using a lightbulb metaphor is not lost on me.

USE.text: I tap the poster. The red glow is just a trick of the lighting. Or maybe the clinic is running on Optimism. The L2, not the emotion.

TALK.text: I read the fine print aloud: 'Side effects of bright ideas may include seed phrase generation, whitepaper writing, and spontaneous DAO formation.'

PICK.text: I try to peel it off. It's laminated AND glued. The clinic protects its motivational content like whales protect their wallets.

OPEN.text: There's nothing behind it. Just a wall. Disappointing, like opening a mystery box NFT and getting a JPEG of a rock.

CLOSE.text: I smooth the poster back flat. The red lightbulb glows ominously. Like a liquidation warning.

PUSH.text: I push the poster. The wall behind it is solid concrete. More reliable than any stablecoin.

PULL.text: I peel back a corner. Someone wrote in pencil behind it: 'The real rug was the friends we made along the way.'

GIVE.text: I have nothing to offer a poster. My portfolio is flatter than this wall.

LOOK.text: An old CRT monitor showing a green line chart trending upward. The eternal hopium machine. Even the clinic's screensaver is bullish.

USE.text: I jiggle the mouse. The screen shows a patient management system. All fields say 'ENCRYPTED'. Even their software has trust issues.

TALK.text: 'Hey computer, show me the patient records.' It responds: 'INSUFFICIENT PERMISSIONS. Try being a doctor or at least a whale.' Rude.

PICK.text: I'm not stealing a whole computer. I'm a crypto detective, not a hardware wallet thief.

OPEN.text: The computer case is screwed shut. Last person who opened it probably voided the warranty AND their position.

CLOSE.text: I close the browser tab. It reopens immediately. Classic popup behavior. The computer is basically a DeFi protocol.

PUSH.text: I push the monitor. The chart wobbles but the green line stays up. Bullish even under physical duress.

PULL.text: I pull the keyboard closer. The spacebar is worn smooth. Someone has been hitting 'CONFIRM TRANSACTION' a LOT.

GIVE.text: The computer runs on electricity, not donations. Unlike most crypto projects.

giveResponses._default: 'I appreciate the gesture, but I can't accept {item}. Medical ethics. Also, I don't have pockets.'
giveResponses.code_note: 'A code? I deal in diagnoses, not digits. Well... both are digits, technically.'
giveResponses.mystery_envelope: 'Confidential mail? In MY clinic? I have enough secrets to manage, thank you.'
giveResponses.ledger: 'A hardware wallet? The last patient who showed me one cried for forty minutes about gas fees.'
giveResponses.keycard: 'Adrian's keycard... He was here, you know. Before the... incident. Keep it. You'll need it more than I do.'
giveResponses.rubber_duck: 'Ah, rubber duck debugging. A valid therapeutic technique. I prescribe it to all my patients. Most ignore me.'
giveResponses.water_bottle: 'Spring water? From the mountain? That's actually the only clean water source left. The clinic's supply tastes like regret.'
giveResponses.golden_token: 'A golden token? I've seen what these do to people. The dopamine, the obsession, the 3 AM chart-checking. I treat that condition.'
giveResponses.floppy_box: 'Floppy discs in a medical facility? That's either genius archival practice or a HIPAA violation. Possibly both.'
giveResponses.printout: 'Transaction logs? I see these in therapy sessions. Patients bring them like medical records. Technically, they ARE their medical records.'
giveResponses.antenna: 'An antenna? Are you trying to intercept my patient communications? That's... actually quite paranoid. Have a seat.'
giveResponses.sign_in_sheet: He reads it carefully. '...These diagnoses are accurate. Disturbingly accurate. Who wrote these?'
giveResponses.clinic_sign_in_sheet: 'That's my own sign-in sheet. I know what it says. I wrote it. The handwriting gets worse with each patient.'
giveResponses.monkey_sticker: 'A three-headed monkey? Fascinating. In crypto-psychology, that represents the three stages of FOMO. I should write a paper.'
giveResponses.broken_mouse: 'A broken mouse? I see the metaphor. The input device is damaged, so the user can't interact with the system. Deep. Very deep.'
giveResponses.burned_chip: 'A burned AdrianAuctions chip... This is what happens when you run smart contracts at maximum gas. The silicon can't take it.'
giveResponses.server_log: 'Server logs from the basement? I... I thought those were destroyed. Who else knows about these?'
giveResponses.clinic_note: He freezes. 'Where did you find that? That was supposed to stay in the trash. Some things are discarded for a reason.'
giveResponses.clinic_photo: 'That photo... happier times. Before Patient Zero's case changed everything.'
giveResponses.dr_satoshi_badge: 'My own badge? I've been looking for that everywhere! I had to use a sticky note that said DOCTOR on it. Very professional.'
giveResponses.adrian_note: He reads it silently. A long pause. 'He knew. He always knew. The game, the patients, the cure... it was all connected.'
giveResponses.receipt: 'A receipt? Every transaction tells a story. This one says... someone bought too many energy drinks at 2 AM. Relatable.'

useResponses._default: I try using {item} on Dr. Satoshi. He adjusts his glasses. 'That's not how medicine works.'
useResponses.rubber_duck: I squeeze the duck at the doctor. 'Interesting. And how does that make you FEEL?' He's turned the session on me.
useResponses.water_bottle: 'Hydration is important. But using water ON a doctor isn't therapy. It's assault.'
useResponses.golden_token: I wave the token at him. 'Ah yes, the classic crypto-hypnosis technique. Doesn't work on me. I'm vaccinated.'
useResponses.keycard: 'Please don't swipe that near my equipment. The last time someone did, it reformatted the patient database.'
useResponses.antenna: 'Are you trying to improve my bedside manner signal? It's already at maximum. This IS my maximum.'
useResponses.clinic_note: I hold up his own crumpled note. He looks away. 'Some days I believe in the tech. Some days the tech doesn't believe in me.'
useResponses.burned_chip: 'Using a burned chip on a doctor. Are you trying to give me a diagnosis, or are you the one who needs help?'
useResponses.floppy_box: 'Inserting floppies into a doctor is NOT a medical procedure. Despite what the internet says.'

greeting.speaker: Dr. Satoshi
greeting.text: I'm Dr. Satoshi. No, not THAT Satoshi. I run this clinic. We treat all forms of on-chain addiction here.

choices.text: Can I see the treatment room?

patient_zero.speaker: Dr. Satoshi
patient_zero.text: Patient Zero... addicted to minting. Every block, another transaction. Cold wallet detox didn't work. Hardware wallet rehab failed. They escaped through a smart contract exploit in our own admission system. Ironic, really.

choices.text: Can I see the treatment room?

where_patient_zero.speaker: Dr. Satoshi
where_patient_zero.text: If I knew that, I wouldn't need visitors poking around. But the treatment room... that's where we kept their records. Everything about their condition. Their wallet history. Their... descent.

choices.text: Can I see the treatment room?

treatment_room.speaker: Dr. Satoshi
treatment_room.text: Not without proper authorization. That room holds answers you might not want. Trust me. Some patients who went in there... never saw their portfolios the same way again.

vip_access.speaker: Dr. Satoshi
vip_access.text: Ah, a holder. The blockchain doesn't lie. VIP access granted. The treatment room is yours. Be careful what you find in there. Not everyone is ready to face... themselves.

onComplete.text: I hear a click from the treatment room door. The card reader turns green.

goodbye.speaker: Dr. Satoshi
goodbye.text: Take your time. The clinic is always open. Unlike most exchanges during a bank run.

script.text: I slide a floppy into the reception CRT's drive. The green line chart on screen flatlines. Then...
'ADRIANLAB FLOPPY RECOGNIZED. PATIENT DATABASE DECRYPTION: AUTHORIZED.' Wait — my floppy collection is a decryption key?
Patient records scroll by. 'Patient #0042: Chronic Airdrop Dependency. Treatment: forced HODL for 90 days. Status: Relapsed after 6 hours.'
'Patient #0069: Acute Governance Proposal Addiction. Voted on 847 proposals in one week. Treatment: touch grass. Status: Grass has been tokenized. Treatment failed.'
At the end of the list: 'Patient #0000 — REDACTED. See Treatment Room.' The cursor blinks. The computer knows more than it's showing.
MEDICAL RECORDS — Decrypted the patient database
